contests.title: ZerveHack
contests.description: What is Zerve? Zerve is an AI-native data science platform. Start with a question, not a blank script. Zerve's agent writes the code, iterates on errors and runs the analysis while you steer. When you're ready, deploy it as an API or app without leaving the platform. The Challenge Use Zerve to build something original. Analyze a dataset, uncover insights, and ship something others can use: an analysis, an app, an API, or all three. No constraints on topic. No prescribed methodology. Bring a question and see how far you can take it. The best submissions will feel like they couldn't have been built the old way. Manual queries, disconnected tools, endless waiting on the data team. Show us what's possible when AI handles the execution and you drive the direction. Getting Started Navigate to the resources tab to signup and get started!
contests.summary: What is Zerve? Zerve is an AI-native data science platform. Start with a question, not a blank script. Zerve's agent writes the code, iterates on errors and runs the analysis while you steer. When you...

contests.title: Agents Assemble - The Healthcare AI Endgame
contests.description: NOTE: We are not accepting any additional applications or inquiries for judges at this time. Due to the high volume of requests, we will not be responding to messages regarding judging opportunities. Thank you for your interest and understanding. Agents Assemble: The Healthcare AI Endgame Challenge Build Interoperable Healthcare Agents at the Intersection of MCP, A2A, and FHIR. We have all watched the AI in healthcare trailers - the hype, the theories, and the cool demos. But now, we have entered the Endgame. At Prompt Opinion, we define this phase as "The Last Mile" - the critical gap where raw intelligence must be converted into actionable deliverables. To conquer this mile, we cannot work alone. We are proud to launch Agents Assemble, a builder’s competition designed to prove that the power of AI lies in collaboration. Our platform provides the headquarters for this mission, giving you native access to the industry's best tools to build in two distinct ways: you can forge a Superpower (building the MCP tools that fuel the ecosystem), or you can rise up to build a Full Agent (equipping those superpowers to tackle a specific healthcare challenge). The technology is ready. The platform is open. It’s time to assemble. Why Participate? This competition is a launchpad for AI solutions that will power future healthcare workflows. Here is why you should join: Lucrative Cash Prizes: Compete for a total prize pool of $25,000 USD in cash, including a $7,500 Grand Prize. Widespread Visibility: Successful projects will be published in the Prompt Opinion Marketplace, making them discoverable and invokable by organizations across the ecosystem. Develop Skills That Matter: Gain hands-on experience with the Model Context Protocol (MCP) and Agent-to-Agent (A2A) standards for building AI agents. Build Healthcare Ready Solutions: Learn how by using the SHARP extension specs from Prompt Opinion, your tools and agents will seamlessly propagate data context using FHIR through multi-agent call chains. Collaborate and Network: Join a community of like-minded innovators and connect with leaders at Prompt Opinion who are shaping the future of AI infrastructure. Bragging Rights: Beyond the prizes, you will walk away with the pride of having built a functional, standards-compliant AI solution that can integrate directly into a clinician's workspace.
contests.summary: NOTE: We are not accepting any additional applications or inquiries for judges at this time. Due to the high volume of requests, we will not be responding to messages regarding judging opportunities. ...

contests.title: Learning Hackathon: Spec Driven Development
contests.description: About the challenge Devpost is launching a new kind of hackathon — shorter, built around learning, and the first in a series. You know the drill: you tell an AI to build you an app, and it kind of works, but the code's a mess and you're not sure what half of it does. Then, when you try to make a big improvement, you end up scrapping it and starting over from scratch. That's what happens when you don't know the best practices of building with AI. This hackathon teaches you spec-driven development — a better way to build with AI. Install a plugin inside Claude Code, plan your app with an AI learning partner, build it, and submit. Most people finish in 4–6 hours. No prizes, no judges — just skills you'll actually use (and a new Devpost badge!). Whether you're learning to code or shipping production software, the plugin will meet you where you are. GETTING STARTED Visit our Resources page for full setup instructions & tutorial on using our Claude Code plugin. Here are also some videos on the process: How to vibe code like a pro Learning plugin walkthrough Claude Code and Plugin Installation You can also access the plugin directly on GitHub at: https://github.com/challengepost/devpost-curriculum A Claude Pro or Max subscription — required to use Claude Code. If you're unsure whether you have one, check before you start. This was the #1 setup surprise in our beta. You'll need to use Claude on your computer! WHY PARTICIPATE Learn by Doing: This isn't a course you watch. You plan, build, and ship a real app — and the process teaches you how to do it again on your own. It's interactive. You'll work inside Claude Code with a custom AI learning partner that guides you through each step — from brainstorming to submission. Think of it less like a tutorial and more like a creative riff session with a smart collaborator. Level Playing Field: No experience requirements beyond the tools listed below. The plugin adapts to your level. Keep the Artifacts: Walk away with a scope document, a requirements doc, a technical spec, a build checklist, and a working app. These are real workflows you can reuse — treat them as templates for your future projects. What You'll Learn Spec-driven development — planning with AI before building, so your projects are intentional instead of generic Flipped interaction prompting — making the AI interview you, not the other way around Context management — keeping your AI productive across sessions instead of starting from scratch every time Verification habits — checking your work at each step so you don't get out over your skis
contests.summary: About the challenge Devpost is launching a new kind of hackathon — shorter, built around learning, and the first in a series. You know the drill: you tell an AI to build you an app, and it kind of wor...

contests.title: Frostbyte Hackathon
contests.description: Frostbyte Hackathon Grand Finale 2026 is the concluding stage of a large-scale, community-driven innovation challenge, bringing together developers, builders, and technology enthusiasts to create impactful solutions across multiple domains. This edition is designed as a refinement-focused and execution-driven round, where participants are encouraged to strengthen their ideas, enhance functionality, and deliver well-structured, real-world solutions. The focus is on building projects that demonstrate clarity, usability, and practical value. The event provides a collaborative environment where participants can explore technologies, iterate on their ideas, and present solutions that address real-world challenges. It is open to individuals and teams from diverse technical and academic backgrounds, encouraging innovation across skill levels. Key highlights of the hackathon include: A structured and transparent evaluation process Opportunities to enhance and improve project quality Recognition across multiple domains Community engagement and knowledge sharing Participants will work on projects spanning areas such as software development, artificial intelligence, data systems, business solutions, and emerging technologies. The Grand Finale serves as a platform to showcase refined work, demonstrate technical capability, and present solutions with strong real-world applicability. Hackathon Themes Participants may build projects across the following domains: Software Development & Engineering Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning Data Analytics & Big Data AI-Driven Decision Systems Finance & Financial Technology Finance & Tax Technology Sales & Marketing Technology Business Management Tools Business Development Platforms General Business Innovation Design & Manufacturing Innovation Cybersecurity & Privacy IoT & Smart Systems Healthcare & BioTech Sustainability & Climate Technology
contests.summary: Frostbyte Hackathon Grand Finale 2026 is the concluding stage of a large-scale, community-driven innovation challenge, bringing together developers, builders, and technology enthusiasts to create impa...

contests.title: Build with MeDo Hackathon
contests.description: Welcome to the Build with MeDo Hackathon！ Whether you’re a developer or a creator, MeDo lets you turn conversations into fully functional applications – from brainstorming to deployment. With MeDo, you can: Build full‑stack apps just by describing them in natural language. Iterate quickly via multi‑turn chat or visual editor. Integrate plugins and APIs effortlessly. Deploy with one click to a public URL. Pick one of our themed tracks or go freestyle. Show us what you can create when AI handles the heavy lifting! Get Started Register with this invite link to get 300 bonus credits! Join our Discord community to connect with other participants, find teammates, and win a $500 community exclusive prize! Community Voting (April 9, 9AM – June 4, 10AM): Post your project in the #showcase channel on Discord. Community members vote for their favorite projects. The 5 projects with the most votes each win $500. More Resources to help you build!
contests.summary: Welcome to the Build with MeDo Hackathon！ Whether you’re a developer or a creator, MeDo lets you turn conversations into fully functional applications – from brainstorming to deployment. With MeDo, yo...

contests.title: Hack Trek
contests.description: 🚀 Hackathon Site – Main Description 🤖 AI Automate 2026 – Build the Future with Machine Learning & Automation Welcome to AI Automate 2026, a high-intensity innovation sprint focused on solving real-world problems using Machine Learning, AI Agents, and Intelligent Automation. This hackathon challenges participants to build systems that can think, learn, decide, and act autonomously. Whether it's: 🤖 AI Agents automating workflows 📊 ML models solving real-world prediction problems 🧠 LLM-powered assistants 🔄 RPA + AI integrations 📡 Smart IoT automation 🏦 AI in fintech, healthcare, cybersecurity, or sustainability We want solutions that reduce human effort, improve decision-making, and scale impact.

contests.title: AlgoFest Hackathon 2026
contests.description: What truly sets ALGOfest Hackathon apart is its focus on blending algorithmic excellence with real-world applications. Unlike traditional hackathons that focus solely on product development, ALGOfest emphasizes the importance of strong computational logic, optimization, scalability, and efficiency. Participants are encouraged not only to build working prototypes but also to design solutions that are robust, efficient, and capable of handling real-world complexity. Another unique aspect is the mentorship-driven approach. Throughout the hackathon, participants gain access to industry experts, experienced developers, and domain specialists who provide guidance, feedback, and insights. This mentorship ensures that teams are not only building projects but also learning and improving continuously during the event. Themes and Tracks ALGOfest 2026 features multiple tracks designed to cater to diverse interests and skill sets: Artificial Intelligence & Machine LearningBuild intelligent systems using cutting-edge AI models, predictive analytics, and automation tools. Sustainable TechnologyCreate solutions addressing climate change, renewable energy, waste management, and environmental sustainability. FinTech InnovationsDevelop secure, scalable financial systems, blockchain applications, and digital payment solutions. HealthTechDesign healthcare solutions that improve accessibility, diagnosis, patient care, and medical data analysis. Smart Cities & IoTBuild smart infrastructure solutions using IoT, data analytics, and automation. Open Innovation TrackFreedom to build anything innovative that doesn’t fit within predefined tracks. Event Format ALGOfest 2026 will be conducted in a hybrid format, allowing both in-person and virtual participation. This ensures inclusivity and accessibility for participants from different geographical locations. The hackathon spans 48 to 72 hours, during which teams brainstorm, design, develop, and present their projects. The event includes: Opening Ceremony Problem Statement Release Team Formation Sessions Mentorship Rounds Workshops & Tech Talks Midway Evaluations Final Presentations Judging & Awards Ceremony Who Should Participate ALGOfest welcomes participants from all backgrounds No matter your skill level, if you have the passion to build and learn, ALGOfest is for you. Why Participate Participants will gain: Hands-on experience solving real-world problems Exposure to cutting-edge technologies Networking opportunities with industry experts Mentorship and guidance Exciting prizes and recognition Internship and career opportunities ALGOfest is not just about winning—it is about learning, collaborating, and growing.
contests.summary: What truly sets ALGOfest Hackathon apart is its focus on blending algorithmic excellence with real-world applications. Unlike traditional hackathons that focus solely on product development, ALGOfest ...

contests.title: GNEC Hackathon 2026 Spring - Compete For UN-Affiliated/NGO Internships & Prizes
contests.description: About the hackathon General The Global NGO Executive Committee (GNEC) and its 1,600 subsidiaries have come together to host our fourth GNEC Hackathon! We encourage those passionate about engineering, technology, and programming to join this event to demonstrate their skills while competing for prizes such as internships and mentorships with our partners. Why Us GNEC is well-connected to the UN and has a global network of NGOs. You can tap into this network during and after this hackathon. Our partners will also host various workshops where they will teach new technological concepts while also offering insight into their mission and the work they have done. The workshops are a great opportunity to explore one’s interests and possible career paths. Theme Through the GNEC Hackathon, we encourage participants to explore sustainability through technology by setting the hackathon's theme as one or two of the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals (UN SDGs). This hackathon's theme will be SDG 3 (Health and Well-being). Get started Join our Discord server (https://discord.gg/n5pcCHcGC6) and meet with everyone else involved in the hackathon. You are welcome to make your own team but if you don't have teammates, our Discord server is a great place to look for a team. The opening ceremony will provide further information regarding the hackathon and the working period will commence shortly after. Be creative, make new friends, and above all, have fun!
contests.summary: About the hackathon General The Global NGO Executive Committee (GNEC) and its 1,600 subsidiaries have come together to host our fourth GNEC Hackathon! We encourage those passionate about engineering, ...

contests.title: EnviroCast Global Engineering Outlook: From Insight to Impact Hackathon
contests.description: About the challenge EnviroCast GEO: From Insight to Impact Hackathon is an international, student-led online hackathon that invites participants to innovate, build, and solve real-world problems to shape the future. Whether you’re creating a web app, mobile tool, hardware prototype, AI solution, or entirely new technology, this hackathon is open-ended — your ideas are the limit. Theme: From Insight to Impact Explore bold engineering solutions Experiment with creative technology Design projects that have real-world impact Ensure projects are efficient and transparent Participants may use Featherless inference credits during the hackathon. Featherless is providing inference access for all participants for the full duration of the hackathon. For a detailed guide on accessing these credits, refer to the Resources section of this hackathon page. All participants will receive 300 credits for MeDo.dev, with technical support and Q&A information through the Discord server. For a detailed guide on accessing these credits, refer to the Resouces section of this hackathon page. Hackathon Highlights: Fully virtual and international Winners announced live during the EnviroCast GEO: Furthering the Future Conference @ http://geo.envirocast.org/ (May 24, Day 2) Winners will also be announced on Devpost If you desire to be a speaker at the GEO international conference, apply at the link provided to be a speaker. Free to enter, open to all eligible students About our Sponsors: Featherless is an inference provider and AI research lab that hosts over 20,000+ open-weight models. Featherless optimizes inference and architecture so models run faster, cheaper, and reliably in production. MeDo is an affordable yet powerful way to turn ideas into life fast, with no code, no setup, no limits. MeDo uses agentic AI to build production‑ready full‑stack apps — frontend, backend, database and integrations — in minutes. Everything is automated through natural language. TruthInTech is a global policy initative advocating for integrity in AI systems through youth-led policy frameworks, global education, and evidence-based governance. Get ready to build, innovate, and showcase your ideas to the world!
contests.summary: About the challenge EnviroCast GEO: From Insight to Impact Hackathon is an international, student-led online hackathon that invites participants to innovate, build, and solve real-world problems to sh...

contests.title: DSH Hacks V1
contests.description: About the Challenge DSH Hacks V1 is a global student competition open to students aged 13+ hosted collaboratively by three youth-led organizations: DeltaForge Hacks, NextHorizon, and STEMise. Theme: AI x STEM Education This event brings together young innovators from around the world to reimagine the future of STEM education through artificial intelligence. Participants will work individually or in teams to design and develop a meaningful technical product that leverages AI to improve, enhance, or expand STEM education. Projects may range from machine learning tools that personalize learning pathways to computer vision systems for laboratory simulations, to AI-driven platforms that increase accessibility for underrepresented communities. Creativity, feasibility, technical depth, and real-world impact will be central to evaluation. The theme of this competition is to create tools that help students learn STEM more effectively, interactively, and accessibly. These tools can be an app, a website, a simulation, a system, or an algorithm. Participants are allowed to use AI to encourage them to build a meaningful solution in the realm of STEM education and to lower the technical barrier that imposes challenges for beginners with ambitious project ideas. Get Started Ask around and form a team! We highly recommend finding like-minded individuals around you to help make your ideas a reality! Please join these Discord servers: DeltaForge Hacks & STEMise! You may very well find your new team!
